TIP_TEXT: B-M-M: Browser -> Brain (Cloud Services) -> Muscle (Compute) -> Memory (Storage)
Micro-partitions: columnar, compressed, immutable, 50-500MB. You CANNOT control their size.
Cloud Services = brain. Handles auth, optimizer, metadata, transactions. Billed only if >10% of daily warehouse credits.
Caching order: Result cache (24h, free) -> Local disk (warehouse SSD) -> Remote disk (storage layer).
Snowpark runs in COMPUTE layer, NOT Cloud Services. UDFs and stored procs also run in compute.
Editions ladder: Standard -> Enterprise -> Business Critical -> VPS. Each adds features, never removes.
Role hierarchy: ACCOUNTADMIN > SECURITYADMIN > USERADMIN + SYSADMIN. ACCOUNTADMIN = SECURITYADMIN + SYSADMIN.
SECURITYADMIN owns MANAGE GRANTS. USERADMIN creates users/roles. SYSADMIN owns warehouses/databases.
Masking policies: ONE policy per column. Enterprise Edition+ required. Attached via ALTER TABLE.
ACCOUNT_USAGE: 45min-3h latency, 365 days retention. INFORMATION_SCHEMA: real-time, 7 days to 6 months retention.
Resource monitors track CREDIT usage, not query count. Can set at ACCOUNT or WAREHOUSE level.
Network policies: ALLOWED_IP_LIST (whitelist) + BLOCKED_IP_LIST (blacklist). Most restrictive wins.
Stage types: User (@~), Table (@%table), Named internal (@stage), Named external (@ext_stage).
PUT command = INTERNAL stages ONLY. For external stages, upload to cloud storage directly.
COPY INTO = bulk batch. Snowpipe = continuous micro-batch (serverless, event-driven).
Snowpipe uses Cloud Services compute, NOT a warehouse. Billed per-file.
File formats mnemonic: CAJ-OPX (CSV, Avro, JSON, ORC, Parquet, XML).
VALIDATION_MODE: checks data WITHOUT loading. RETURN_ERRORS | RETURN_N_ROWS | RETURN_ALL_ERRORS.
Cache check order: Result cache (free, 24h) -> Local disk (warehouse SSD) -> Remote disk (storage).
Spilling = warehouse too small -> queries spill to local disk, then remote. Solution: scale UP.
Queuing = not enough clusters -> queries wait. Solution: scale OUT (multi-cluster).
Economy scaling = save credits (SOS). Standard scaling = reduce latency (QAS). Know which to pick!
Pruning uses micro-partition min/max metadata. ORDER BY on filter columns -> better pruning.
FLATTEN converts VARIANT/ARRAY/OBJECT -> rows. Usually paired with LATERAL in FROM clause.
Shares are READ-ONLY. Consumer cannot modify. Provider pays storage, consumer pays compute.
Reader accounts: provider creates AND pays for compute. For consumers without Snowflake.
Secure views hide query definition. REQUIRED for shares -- regular views expose the SQL.
Time Travel: 0-1 day (Standard), 0-90 days (Enterprise). Then 7 days Fail-safe (Snowflake only).
Cloning = zero-copy = metadata-only at creation. Privileges are NOT cloned.
Data Clean Rooms: secure multi-party analysis without exposing raw data to each other.
Tri-Secret Secure: customer-managed key + Snowflake key + composite master key. Business Critical+.
Network policies apply at ACCOUNT or USER level. CIDR notation required.
SCIM = automated user provisioning. Supports Azure AD, Okta. SECURITYADMIN role required.
Private Link: AWS PrivateLink / Azure Private Link. No data over public internet.
Key pair authentication: 2048-bit RSA minimum. Can have 2 active keys for rotation.
Global Services layer handles account replication, failover, database replication across regions.
Hybrid tables: transactional + analytical. Row-store index for low-latency OLTP lookups.
Snowgrid: cross-cloud, cross-region data sharing and replication framework.
Replication groups: bundle databases, shares, and other objects for coordinated failover.
Client redirect: automatic client failover between primary and secondary connections.
Cross-region sharing requires database replication first -- cannot share directly across regions.
Auto-Fulfillment handles cross-cloud Marketplace delivery automatically. Manual replication for direct shares.
Data Clean Rooms: secure multi-party analysis without exposing raw data.
Reader accounts: provider pays EVERYTHING (storage + compute). Read-only access.
Secure views REQUIRED for sharing -- regular views expose the SQL definition.
Dynamic Tables: declarative pipelines. Set TARGET_LAG, Snowflake auto-refreshes. Replaces streams+tasks.
Streams + Tasks: CDC pattern. Stream captures changes, Task runs on schedule or trigger.
Snowpipe Streaming: Java/Python SDK, sub-second latency. No staging files needed.
Apache Iceberg tables: open format, Snowflake-managed or externally managed catalog.
Data sharing + Marketplace: zero-copy, live data. Provider pays storage, consumer pays compute.
Query Profile: focus on TableScan (pruning), Sort (spilling), Aggregate (grouping).
Search Optimization Service: point lookups, substring search, geo functions. Enterprise+.
Materialized Views: auto-refreshed, automatic query rewrite. Enterprise+. Best for heavy aggregations.
Clustering keys: large tables (multi-TB), frequently filtered columns. Check SYSTEM$CLUSTERING_INFORMATION.
Multi-cluster warehouse: Standard policy (perf) vs Economy policy (cost). Auto-scale 1-10 clusters.
Shares are READ-ONLY. Consumer cannot modify. Provider pays storage, consumer pays compute.
Secure views REQUIRED for sharing -- regular views expose the SQL definition to consumers.
Reader accounts: provider creates AND pays for compute. For consumers without Snowflake accounts.
Cross-region sharing requires database replication first -- cannot share directly across regions.
Data Clean Rooms: secure multi-party analysis without exposing raw data to each other.
Native Apps (Application Packages): distribute code + data as installable packages via Marketplace.
Zero-copy clones for dev/staging/prod environments. Cloned tasks are suspended by default.
SPCS (Snowpark Container Services): run Docker containers in Snowflake compute. GPU support.
Snowpark: DataFrame API in Python/Java/Scala. Runs in Snowflake compute, not client-side.
CI/CD: use SnowCLI or Terraform for infrastructure-as-code. SchemaChange for migration scripts.
Environment isolation: separate databases or accounts per environment. Use managed access schemas.
Raw -> Transform -> Consumption zone pattern for data pipeline architecture.
COPY INTO = bulk batch. Snowpipe = continuous serverless. Snowpipe Streaming = sub-second via SDK.
Snowpipe uses Snowflake-managed serverless compute, NOT your warehouse. Billed per-second.
AUTO_INGEST requires cloud event notifications: S3 SQS, GCS Pub/Sub, or Azure Event Grid.
Load metadata stored 64 days. After that, same files can reload without FORCE = TRUE.
Storage Integration decouples cloud credentials from stage definitions. Uses IAM roles.
Schema Evolution (ENABLE_SCHEMA_EVOLUTION) only ADDS columns. Never modifies or drops existing ones.
Cache order: Result cache (24h, free) -> Local disk (warehouse SSD) -> Remote disk (storage layer).
Spilling = warehouse too small -> scale UP. Queuing = not enough clusters -> scale OUT.
Clustering keys: best for large tables (multi-TB) with frequent range/equality filters on specific columns.
Search Optimization Service: point lookups, substring search, geo functions. Enterprise+ required.
Query Acceleration Service: offloads portions of eligible queries to shared compute. Good for outlier queries.
Resource monitors track CREDIT usage. Can notify or suspend at account or warehouse level.
Time Travel: 0-1 day Standard, 0-90 days Enterprise. Then 7-day Fail-safe (Snowflake only, not queryable).
Transient tables: 0-1 day Time Travel, NO Fail-safe. Temporary tables: same, session-scoped.
Cloning = zero-copy at creation (metadata only). New writes use separate storage. Privileges NOT cloned.
UNDROP works within Time Travel retention. Order matters: most recently dropped restored first.
Micro-partitions: columnar, compressed, immutable, 50-500MB. You cannot control their size directly.
RBAC: privileges granted to roles, roles granted to users. DAC: object owner controls access.
Masking policies: ONE per column. Enterprise+ required. Attached via ALTER TABLE ... SET MASKING POLICY.
Row Access Policies: ONE per table/view. Controls which rows users see based on context functions.
Object tagging: key-value metadata for classification. Tag-based masking = masking policy attached to a tag.
ACCOUNTADMIN = top role. SECURITYADMIN manages grants. USERADMIN creates users/roles. SYSADMIN owns objects.
Streams capture DML changes (inserts, updates, deletes). SYSTEM$STREAM_HAS_DATA() checks for new data.
Tasks run on schedule (CRON or interval) or via AFTER triggers (task trees/DAGs).
Dynamic Tables: declarative pipelines with TARGET_LAG. Snowflake auto-refreshes. Replaces streams+tasks for many use cases.
UDFs return values inline in queries. Stored procedures execute procedural logic and can use SQL, JavaScript, Python, Java.
FLATTEN + LATERAL: expand semi-structured arrays/objects into rows. Essential for JSON/VARIANT processing.
Snowpark DataFrame API: Python/Java/Scala. Executes in Snowflake compute, not client-side.

DOMAIN_NAME: Domain 1: Snowflake for Gen AI Overview
TIP_TEXT: Cortex suite: COMPLETE, Cortex Search, Cortex Analyst, Cortex Fine-tuning, Cortex Agents (Preview).

DOMAIN_NAME: Domain 1: Snowflake for Gen AI Overview
TIP_TEXT: CORTEX_MODELS_ALLOWLIST: account parameter to restrict which LLM models users can call.

DOMAIN_NAME: Domain 1: Snowflake for Gen AI Overview
TIP_TEXT: Interfaces: SQL, REST API, Cortex LLM Playground (Preview). Know when to use each.

DOMAIN_NAME: Domain 1: Snowflake for Gen AI Overview
TIP_TEXT: Bring your own models via Model Registry (custom model logging) or Hugging Face on SPCS.

DOMAIN_NAME: Domain 1: Snowflake for Gen AI Overview
TIP_TEXT: Vector types: VECTOR_COSINE_SIMILARITY, VECTOR_L2_DISTANCE, VECTOR_INNER_PRODUCT, VECTOR_L1_DISTANCE.

DOMAIN_NAME: Domain 1: Snowflake for Gen AI Overview
TIP_TEXT: Cross-region inference: CORTEX_ENABLED_CROSS_REGION parameter. Consider latency vs availability tradeoffs.

DOMAIN_NAME: Domain 2: Snowflake Gen AI & LLM Functions
TIP_TEXT: COMPLETE = general LLM. Task-specific: CLASSIFY_TEXT, EXTRACT_ANSWER, SENTIMENT, SUMMARIZE, TRANSLATE.

DOMAIN_NAME: Domain 2: Snowflake Gen AI & LLM Functions
TIP_TEXT: TRY_COMPLETE: returns NULL on failure instead of aborting query. COUNT_TOKENS: varies by model tokenizer.

DOMAIN_NAME: Domain 2: Snowflake Gen AI & LLM Functions
TIP_TEXT: SPLIT_TEXT_RECURSIVE_CHARACTER: chunk text for embedding. Overlapping chunks preserve context.

DOMAIN_NAME: Domain 2: Snowflake Gen AI & LLM Functions
TIP_TEXT: Cortex Analyst: text-to-SQL via semantic model (YAML in stage or semantic views). VQR = Verified Query Repository.

DOMAIN_NAME: Domain 2: Snowflake Gen AI & LLM Functions
TIP_TEXT: Chat: multi-turn architecture needs conversation memory. Build with Streamlit + COMPLETE.

DOMAIN_NAME: Domain 2: Snowflake Gen AI & LLM Functions
TIP_TEXT: SPCS for third-party models: Docker image → image repository → compute pool → service. Model Registry for logging/calling.

DOMAIN_NAME: Domain 2: Snowflake Gen AI & LLM Functions
TIP_TEXT: Cortex Search: hybrid search (semantic + keyword). ATTRIBUTES = metadata for filtering, not searchable content.
Cortex Guard: filter harmful/unsafe LLM responses. Reduce hallucination with RAG grounding.
Cost views: CORTEX_FUNCTIONS_USAGE_HISTORY, CORTEX_FUNCTIONS_QUERY_USAGE_HISTORY, METERING_DAILY_HISTORY.
CORTEX_SEARCH_DAILY_USAGE_HISTORY: three cost types — warehouse, EMBED_TEXT, serving.
AI Observability (Preview): evaluation metrics, tracing, logging via event tables. Trulens SDK integration.
Minimize costs: choose smaller models when possible, reduce token count, use task-specific functions over COMPLETE.
REST API auth: key pair, OAuth. Data never leaves Snowflake boundary when using Cortex functions.
